<<THE SPRINTER'S MOVEMENT IS MODELLED BY A FUNCTION V(t) = AT³ - BT² + CT + D>>  
<<THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS, AND IS 12 METRES PER SECOND>>  
<<THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY IS THE DERIVATIVE OF V(t) AT T = 3, SO V'(3) = 0>>  
<<THE DERIVATIVE OF V(t) IS 3AT² - 2BT + C, AND THIS EQUALS ZERO AT T = 3>>  
<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C IS 3A(3)² - 2B(3) + C = 0>>  
<<THE RELATIONSHIP IS 27A - 6B + C = 0>>  
<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS THE TOTAL DISTANCE COVERED DIVIDED BY THE TIME TAKEN>>  
<<THE TOTAL DISTANCE IS THE INTEGRAL OF V(t) FROM T = 0 TO T = 5>>  
<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS THE INTEGRAL OF V(t) FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 DIVIDED BY 5>>  
<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS W(5) - W(0) OVER 5>>  
<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS W(5) - W(0) OVER 5>>  
<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS W(5) - W(0) OVER 5>>
